criteria.label: Hook Strength
criteria.description: The first 1-2 seconds create immediate visual or narrative interest.

criteria.label: Product Clarity
criteria.description: The viewer can understand what is being promoted.

criteria.label: Visual Specificity
criteria.description: Scenes include concrete materials, environments, actions, and camera intent.

criteria.label: Campaign Ambition
criteria.description: The campaign has a specific creative spine, status change, and memorable directed event.

criteria.label: Concept Collision
criteria.description: The selected premise uses a forced collision such as wrong genre, sound-first, ugly-beautiful, boring-on-purpose, single-take, or audience ritual instead of default ad grammar.

criteria.label: Reference DNA
criteria.description: One named direction DNA is translated into camera, light, blocking, performance, edit pace, and forbidden moves without copying protected expression.

criteria.label: Aesthetic Refusals
criteria.description: The treatment and prompts name specific taste moves the campaign refuses, separate from artifact controls.

criteria.label: Controlled Imperfection
criteria.description: A deliberate flaw makes the campaign feel directed and human without becoming a model artifact.

criteria.label: Sound Spine
criteria.description: Music or silence shapes the edit through a beat ladder when sound-first is selected, and supports pacing when visual-led.

criteria.label: Fashion/Product Authority
criteria.description: Styling, silhouette, material behavior, posture, product truth, and body language feel intentional.

criteria.label: Editorial Progression
criteria.description: The edit escalates through meaningful changes instead of interchangeable coverage.

criteria.label: Final Memory Image
criteria.description: The final image feels earned, specific, and memorable.

criteria.label: Motion Design
criteria.description: Camera and subject motion support the idea instead of creating noise.

criteria.label: Continuity
criteria.description: Product, character, environment, lighting, and color remain consistent.

criteria.label: Artifact Control
criteria.description: The result avoids anatomy, reflection, text, logo, and product-geometry failures.

criteria.label: Imagine.Art Workflow Completeness
criteria.description: The workflow includes inputs, analysis, stillframes, motion, music, edit assembly, QC, rejects, and final export planning.

criteria.label: Music Fit
criteria.description: The Imagine.Art Music Studio direction supports pacing, mood, cuts, and final campaign tone.

criteria.label: Public Safety
criteria.description: The package avoids private, copyrighted, or unauthorized brand/person references.
